chunking_principles: BM25 queries contain only unique, hard nouns and technical terms.
Vector queries use fluent, sentence-level descriptions for bge-small optimization.
Expanded texts are declarative True/False rubrics for the Cross-Encoder.

chunks.bm25_query: search ranking retrieval recommendation matching engine relevance intelligence layer
chunks.vector_query: Builds and owns the intelligence layer for semantic search, talent recommendation, and relevance ranking engines.
chunks.expanded_text: The candidate explicitly states they have built or engineered ranking, retrieval, recommendation, or search relevance systems.
chunks.notes: Captures the JD's central product mandate only; shipped-system proof belongs in C05.

chunks.bm25_query: production embeddings retrieval deployed sentence transformers BGE E5 OpenAI vectorization dense vectors semantic similarity representation learning text-embedding-ada SBERT MiniLM
chunks.vector_query: Deploys embeddings-based retrieval, semantic search, and dense retrievers using models like sentence-transformers, OpenAI embeddings, or BGE to real users.
chunks.expanded_text: The candidate has explicit production experience building embeddings-based retrieval systems that reached real users.
chunks.notes: Highest-priority core requirement focused on deployed embedding retrieval.

chunks.bm25_query: vector database hybrid search Pinecone Weaviate Qdrant Milvus OpenSearch Elasticsearch FAISS ANN HNSW Chroma pgvector Vespa Solr
chunks.vector_query: Operates and scales vector databases, approximate nearest-neighbor search, and hybrid retrieval infrastructure like Pinecone, Weaviate, Milvus, or FAISS.
chunks.expanded_text: The candidate has production experience operating and scaling vector databases or hybrid search infrastructure.

chunks.bm25_query: embedding drift index refresh retrieval quality regression production operations versioning reindexing recall precision relevance monitoring evaluation dashboard
chunks.vector_query: Handles production retrieval operations including embedding drift, index refresh, retrieval-quality regression, and recall monitoring.
chunks.expanded_text: The candidate operates retrieval systems post-launch, actively managing embedding drift, index refresh, and retrieval-quality regressions.
chunks.notes: Separates operational retrieval maintenance from merely having used embeddings.

chunks.bm25_query: shipped end to end ranking search recommendation system real users production launched deployed recommender matching engine relevance service
chunks.vector_query: Ships end-to-end ranking, retrieval, search, recommendation, or matching systems from design through production launch for real users.
chunks.expanded_text: The candidate has explicitly taken a ranking, search, recommendation, or matching system from design through launch for real users.

chunks.bm25_query: NDCG MRR MAP relevance benchmark ranking metrics evaluation Recall Precision HitRate DCG ERR qrels
chunks.vector_query: Evaluates ranking systems using metrics and benchmarks like NDCG, MRR, MAP, Recall, Precision, and rigorous offline relevance measurement.
chunks.expanded_text: The candidate actively uses formal ranking and search evaluation metrics such as NDCG, MRR, MAP, or relevance labeling.
chunks.notes: Metric knowledge only; experimentation loops belong in C07.

chunks.bm25_query: offline online correlation A B testing feedback loops experimentation infrastructure experiments interleaving click human labeling CTR conversion
chunks.vector_query: Builds experimentation and feedback loops connecting offline relevance benchmarks with online A/B tests, interleaving, and click signals.
chunks.expanded_text: The candidate builds evaluation infrastructure linking offline benchmarks to online A/B tests and engagement metrics.

chunks.bm25_query: strong Python production code quality hands on ML systems implementation FastAPI PyTorch scikit-learn sklearn pandas numpy backend API
chunks.vector_query: Writes strong Python and production-quality ML systems code, including backend APIs and PyTorch or scikit-learn pipelines.
chunks.expanded_text: The candidate writes production-quality Python code and builds hands-on ML systems and backend APIs.
chunks.notes: Captures the JD's explicit emphasis that this role writes code.

chunks.bm25_query: systems thinking architecture observability pipelines infrastructure tradeoff reasoning monitoring logging tracing SLO SLA reliability scalability orchestration Airflow Kubernetes Docker
chunks.vector_query: Thinks in systems, architecture, observability, pipelines, reliability, scalability, orchestration, and engineering tradeoffs.
chunks.expanded_text: The candidate demonstrates deep systems-thinking, focusing on architecture, observability, infrastructure design, and engineering tradeoffs.

chunks.bm25_query: ship fast working v1 real users iterate learn product engineering MVP prototype launch user feedback agile rapid experimentation
chunks.vector_query: Ships fast, launches practical MVP product systems, learns from real users, and iterates quickly with strong product engineering judgment.
chunks.expanded_text: The candidate has a shipper mindset, willing to launch a working v1 quickly and iterate based on real user feedback.

chunks.bm25_query: recruiter workflow candidate matching talent intelligence marketplace ATS sourcing talent acquisition hiring resume screening recommendation
chunks.vector_query: Understands recruiter workflows, sourcing, ATS hiring funnels, resume screening, talent intelligence, and marketplace matching context.
chunks.expanded_text: The candidate has built products for recruiter workflows, talent intelligence, applicant tracking systems, or hiring marketplaces.
chunks.notes: Narrowed to the JD's matching and recruiter-workflow context.

chunks.bm25_query: ownership 0 to 1 ambiguity founding team build from scratch imperfect specs greenfield early stage zero to one startup product discovery
chunks.vector_query: Takes ownership in ambiguous zero-to-one, greenfield, startup, or founding-team environments with imperfect specs.
chunks.expanded_text: The candidate operates autonomously in zero-to-one founding environments, building from scratch with highly ambiguous requirements.

chunks.bm25_query: LLM reranking based re ranking pipeline integration GenAI Generative AI RAG Retrieval Augmented Generation OpenAI Claude Llama Mistral LangChain HuggingFace agentic workflow cross encoder
chunks.vector_query: Uses LLM-based reranking, GenAI, RAG, agentic workflows, or LLM integration with OpenAI, Claude, Llama, or LangChain inside retrieval pipelines.
chunks.expanded_text: The candidate implements LLM-based reranking, RAG, or agentic workflows as part of a broader retrieval or ranking pipeline.

chunks.bm25_query: async first writing open disagreement decide quickly written reasoning documentation design docs RFCs memos communication remote collaboration
chunks.vector_query: Works async-first, writes clearly in docs or RFCs, reasons in writing, collaborates remotely, and decides quickly.
chunks.expanded_text: The candidate communicates complex technical tradeoffs clearly through asynchronous writing, RFCs, and design documents.

chunks.bm25_query: LLM fine tuning LoRA QLoRA PEFT instruction adapters transformers HuggingFace TRL SFT supervised
chunks.vector_query: Has LLM fine-tuning or instruction-tuning experience with LoRA, QLoRA, PEFT, adapters, HuggingFace transformers, or SFT.
chunks.expanded_text: The candidate fine-tunes large language models using parameter-efficient methods like LoRA, QLoRA, or PEFT.

chunks.bm25_query: learning to rank XGBoost LightGBM neural ranker model LambdaMART LambdaRank RankNet pairwise pointwise listwise features
chunks.vector_query: Has learning-to-rank model experience using LambdaMART, RankNet, XGBoost, LightGBM, neural rankers, and pairwise or listwise objectives.
chunks.expanded_text: The candidate applies classical machine learning models, such as XGBoost or LightGBM, to learning-to-rank problems.

chunks.bm25_query: distributed systems large scale inference optimization latency throughput GPU batching quantization ONNX TensorRT vLLM serving caching autoscaling
chunks.vector_query: Has large-scale inference optimization background covering latency, throughput, batching, GPU serving, quantization, ONNX, TensorRT, and vLLM.
chunks.expanded_text: The candidate optimizes large-scale distributed systems and inference engines for latency, throughput, and GPU serving.

chunks.bm25_query: AI ML open source papers talks visible technical work external validation GitHub publications blogs conference workshops Kaggle writing artifacts
chunks.vector_query: Has positive external validation through visible AI or ML technical work like open-source contributions, papers, talks, or GitHub projects.
chunks.expanded_text: The candidate has a visible track record of external technical validation, such as open-source contributions, academic papers, or conference talks.

chunks.bm25_query: pure research academic lab thesis coursework benchmark
chunks.vector_query: Has a pure research, academic lab, thesis, or benchmark-only profile completely lacking production software deployment.
chunks.expanded_text: Negative evidence that the candidate's experience is STRICTLY limited to pure research, academic labs, or coursework without ANY production software deployment.
chunks.notes: Explicit JD disqualifier focused on lack of production deployment.

chunks.bm25_query: LangChain OpenAI wrapper prompt engineering chatbot demo boilerplate API
chunks.vector_query: Builds basic prompt engineering, chatbot demos, and wrapper-only AI features without deeper retrieval or ranking infrastructure.
chunks.expanded_text: Negative evidence that the candidate's ENTIRE artificial intelligence experience consists of basic wrappers, prompt engineering, or LangChain tutorials without deep retrieval infrastructure.
chunks.notes: Explicit JD disqualifier for wrapper-heavy AI profiles.

chunks.bm25_query: senior architect tech lead hands off
chunks.vector_query: Works as a hands-off manager, pure architect, or director who no longer writes code or builds ML systems directly.
chunks.expanded_text: Negative evidence that the candidate is EXCLUSIVELY a hands-off manager or architect who no longer writes production code.
chunks.notes: Explicit JD risk because this role requires writing code.

chunks.bm25_query: computer vision speech robotics
chunks.vector_query: Focuses technical expertise entirely on computer vision, speech, or robotics without any NLP, information retrieval, or ranking exposure.
chunks.expanded_text: Negative evidence that the candidate's technical expertise is ENTIRELY focused on computer vision, speech, or robotics, with absolutely no NLP or search experience.

chunks.bm25_query: closed source proprietary systems outsourcing consulting services
chunks.vector_query: Works exclusively in IT Services, Consulting, or outsourcing firms on closed-source proprietary systems.
chunks.expanded_text: Negative evidence that the candidate works EXCLUSIVELY in IT Services, consulting, or outsourcing without core product-company experience.

chunks.bm25_query: framework enthusiast tutorials demos hot toy projects proof of concept PoC notebook Streamlit
chunks.vector_query: Builds framework tutorials, toy projects, notebooks, and Streamlit demos without production systems thinking or architecture depth.
chunks.expanded_text: Negative evidence that the candidate's portfolio consists ENTIRELY of toy projects, bootcamp tutorials, or basic demos lacking production-grade engineering.

chunks.bm25_query: informational ideal candidate summary do not score
chunks.vector_query: informational-only summary of the ideal Redrob AI candidate
chunks.expanded_text: Informational-only ideal candidate summary: applied ML engineer with production retrieval, ranking, evaluation, matching, product-engineering, ownership, and recruiter-workflow judgment. This chunk is for inspection only and must never affect retrieval or ranking scores.
chunks.notes: Weight is zero by design; exclude from BM25, vector retrieval, cross-encoder scoring, and aggregate scoring.

bm25: Use chunk weights to scale lexical retrieval importance for C01 through C24 only.
Exclude C25 from BM25 retrieval because it is informational and has weight 0.0.
Aggregate chunk-level BM25 scores without double counting overlapping evidence listed in do_not_duplicate_with.
Route direct candidate-key and Redrob-signal features to the structured scoring layer, not this JD index.
vector: Use chunk weights to scale semantic similarity importance for C01 through C24 only.
Exclude C25 from vector retrieval because it is informational and has weight 0.0.
Compare chunks only against candidate narrative text such as headline, summary, and career descriptions.
Aggregate chunk similarities with deduplication so one piece of evidence cannot satisfy multiple overlapping chunks.
dedupe: C01 is role intent only, C05 is shipped end-to-end proof only, and C13 is LLM reranking or LLM pipeline integration only.
C06 is ranking metric literacy, while C07 is experimentation, offline-online correlation, and recruiter-feedback infrastructure.
C09 is systems architecture, C10 is shipper behavior, C12 is ambiguity ownership, and C14 is async writing and decision style.
C18 rewards visible external validation, while C23 penalizes closed-source-only work without external proof.

For overlapping chunks that share the same evidence, compute overlap_adjusted as 1.0 times the strongest matching score plus 0.5 times the second matching score plus 0.25 times the third matching score, then ignore any further duplicate matches.
negative_scoring: Negative chunks C19 through C24 use weight as penalty magnitude controlled by polarity.
Apply each negative concept at most once even if multiple lexical forms match.
Do not use structured career-history or platform-activity features as evidence for these JD chunks.
